{{Quote|I would recommend burning off the forest for a hundred kilometers around the generator and putting a small mechanized force of AT-ATs and juggernaut heavy assault vehicles under the umbrella shield. Add in point support from three or four wing-clusters of hoverscouts, and the rest of the troops and equipment could be reassigned to trouble spots elsewhere in the Empire.|Grand Admiral Thrawn's recommendations for defending the shield bunker on Endor|<ref name="CoO">''[[Choices of One]]''</ref>}}
The '''Swift Assault 5''' was a [[hovercraft]] manufactured by the [[Mekuun Corporation/Legends|Mekuun Corporation]].

==Characteristics==
[[Mekuun Corporation/Legends|Mekuun Corporation's]]<ref name="ISB" /> Swift Assault 5<ref name="SE" /> was a 15.9 [[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long [[hovercraft]].<ref name="ISB2" /> It employed four [[Kasmin]] three-stage [[turbopump]]s to generate an air cushion. Kasmin's turbopumps were the most refined heavy-duty air blower of its type available, permitting the Swift Assault 5 to produce extra elevation and float when required. A [[Gurian]] [[S87 "perpetual charge" power plant dynamo]] provided power for the hoverscout; a [[Power capacitor/Legends|power capacitor]] started the air blower rotation, and the energy generated by the vehicles air cushion was recycled via [[back-blast impeller]]s to supplement the S87's power generation. The efficient engine design gave the Swift Assault 5 a longer operational range than equivalent vehicles of its size and type.<ref name="ISB" />

Capable of speeds up to two hundred [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]],<ref name="ISB2" /> the Swift Assault 5's speed and maneuverability were not on par with standard [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles, despite it having being modified from standard hover vehicle specifications to deal with military demands for traversing rough and rugged terrain. The hoverscout was favored on [[Planet/Legends|planets]] where terrain and planetary conditions caused erratic behavior in the operation of repulsorlift engines.<ref name="ISB" />

Swift Assault 5 could be piloted by a single crewmember, but an optimal crew of four allowed all the craft's weapon systems to be employed without loss of efficiency. Training to crew a hoverscout required less equivalent training than was required to crew a repulsorlift vehicle. Six passengers could also be accommodated. Standard [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] packages included weapon-linked [[electro photo receptor]]s at each crew position, as well as long-range communications equipment. Other sensor and communications packages could be installed depending on the mission profile.<ref name="ISB" /> Between fifty<ref name="ISB2" /> and four hundred [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] of cargo could be carried, in five hundred [[cubic centimeter]]s of space.<ref name="DFRSB" /> It could also store consumables for two [[Standard day/Legends|days]].<ref name="SE" />

The Swift Assault 5 had an excellent maintenance and failure rate that was better than other floater scouts of similar type available on the market, and it was cheaper and easier to maintain.<ref name="ISB" /> They could be purchased new for 75,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], and used for between 45,000<ref name="SE" /> and 55,000 credits.<ref name="DFRSB" />

Although lightly-armored,<ref name="DFRSB" /> so as not to impair its maneuverability, the Swift Assault 5 could nevertheless withstand a direct hit. The doors and weapons ports on the vehicle were sealed, giving the hoverscout a fully closed and recyclable interior [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. A wide field of vision for the pilot was achieved through the application of a windscreen made of [[ceraglass]], a ceramic alloy that was both transparent and as tough as armor. The hoverscout was heavily armed, with three main weapons.<ref name="ISB" /> A [[Mk 3/S heavy blaster cannon]] that was turret-mounted, and a light [[Mk 1/S laser cannon]] and [[Mk 2e/S concussion missile launcher]]<ref name="MBC" /> that were able to fire in front and to the sides of the vehicle.<ref name="DFRSB" /> Some models mounted a double blaster cannon and a double laser cannon,<ref name="SE" /> and a payload of either six [[Mk 1/S concussion missile]]s<ref name="MBC" /> or sixteen [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] was carried.<ref name="SE" />

==History==
The Swift Assault 5 was employed on a variety of worlds, deployed by a wide array of ground forces in numerous combat conditions, and proved its worth as an efficient combat vehicle. The [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] typically deployed the hoverscout as a reconnaissance asset, or for offensive point operations, and it was as used in conjunction with other hoverscouts for raiding duty or as part of the rearguard. The hoverscout could be used independently, or with support from either [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] or armor units.<ref name="ISB" />

Shortly after the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] used Swift Assault 5s to patrol [[Prak City]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Prakith/Legends|Prakith]].<ref name="SE" /> Three hoverscouts attempted to stop the [[Mistryl Shadow Guard]]s [[Shada D'ukal]] and [[Karoly D'ulin]] from stealing the Imperial prototype weapon codenamed "[[Hammertong/Legends|Hammertong]]"<ref name="H">{{StoryCite|story=Hammertong: The Tale of the "Tonnika Sisters"|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}</ref> in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> Eight [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] counseled [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to use hoverscouts in conjunction with [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]] and [[juggernaut]]s to defend the [[Endor shield generator bunker]] before realizing that the Emperor intended [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] to be a trap for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="CoO" />

In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],<ref name="ERC" /> the Empire used hoverscouts to patrol the countryside outside of [[Barons Hed]].<ref name="RA">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''</ref> In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="ERC" /> an assault force of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] were backed by a hoverscout when raiding an Alliance operation to switch out computer cores at the [[Palar plant]] on the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Members of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], backed by [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] elements and the [[Alien Combine]], were attempting to insert a doctored core into the computer controlling Coruscant's planetary shield. The operation was sold out by [[Zekka Thyne]].<ref name="WG">''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]''</ref>

During Grand Admiral Thrawn's [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], one of his senior Army commanders, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Freja Covell]], deployed hoverscouts alongside [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]].<ref name="DFRSB" /> They were employed during Thrawn's [[Raid on Myrkr Base|assault]] on [[Talon Karrde]]'s [[Myrkr Base]].<ref name="DFR">''[[Dark Force Rising]]''</ref>
